target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
AIR is a Series A transportation company founded by Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, and Netanel Goldberg, having raised $23M to date. The transportation sector comp universe includes 8 private peers (REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger among them) and no public comparables.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($23M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $46M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while 5 same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap at the same stage, adjacent stages, or similar valuation tier (fewer than 3 datapoints across these filters) makes median-based triangulation unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a median point of $46M. This range reflects reasonable bounds around the stage-progression result, though the narrow methodological foundation—relying on a single applicable technique—creates material uncertainty. Key limitations include the absence of true peer comparables at AIR's stage and valuation tier within transportation, lack of public market benchmarks, and the stage-multiplier assumption's sensitivity to market conditions and AIR's specific growth trajectory. Additional diligence on unit economics, market penetration, and capital efficiency relative to peers would be warranted before final valuation.
